title: Setting up your teacher account and creating a class on Khan Academy
description: A check for understanding of the Setting up your Khan Academy teacher account lesson.
topic_path: Setting up your teacher account and creating a class
Setting up your teacher account and creating a class on Khan Academy

assessment_items.question_content:
Below is an image of a screen.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which screen does this image represent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The is the **teacher dashboard**, the main page for teachers to access tools and reports on Khan Academy.

options.alt: An image of a computer screen with the title, "Welcome, Khan Teacher". Below that it has three subheadings for classes, students and resources. Then in a list, it shows the title of four different classes.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Where would you go to adjust your email address, password, or other aspects of your Khan Academy teacher account?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The **Settings tab** is where you can edit settings such as email address, password, username, and teacher capabilities.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following allows you to create a teacher account and log into Khan Academy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: You can create a teacher account, and log into Khan Academy using **Google**, **Clever**, **Facebook** or an **email address**.

assessment_items.question_content:
Content on Khan Academy is split into two categories: the videos and articles.

**Fill in the blanks.**

Articles are [[☃ dropdown 1]] and the exercises, quizzes and challenges are [[☃ dropdown 2]].
hints: Articles are **instructional content** and the exercises, quizzes and challenges are **practice content**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fill in the blanks.**

A key part of practice on Khan Academy is that students receive  [[☃ dropdown 3]] about how they’re doing, along with [[☃ dropdown 4]] to help them get back on track if they’re not quite clear on a particular concept.
hints: A key part of practice on Khan Academy is that students receive **instant feedback** about how they’re doing, along with **worked solutions** to help them get back on track if they’re not quite clear on a particular concept.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Khan Academy has content in which areas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Khan Academy has content for **Math**, **Science**, **Test Prep**, **History** and more.

assessment_items.question_content:
As students work through content on Khan Academy assesses their skill level. 

**Place the skill levels from least to most progress. **

[[☃ orderer 2]]

**Mastery learning is sometimes used as a synonym for standards-based or competency-based learning.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Fill in the blank.**

[[☃ dropdown 1]] learning can indicate [[☃ dropdown 3]] learning where the things to be mastered are a delineated set of standards.
hints: **Standards-based** learning can indicate **mastery** learning where the things to be mastered are a delineated set of standards.
